problem.title: Permutations
problem.description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
assumptions: Return all permutations in any order.

example.explanation: All 3! = 6 permutations. Backtracking: swap elements into position recursively.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Backtracking
solutions.description: Use backtracking with a boolean used-array to generate all permutations: at each recursive step, try every unused number, mark it used, recurse to fill the next position, then unmark it to restore state. Because all elements are distinct, no deduplication logic is needed.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def permute(nums: List[int]) -> List[List[int]]:
    result: List[List[int]] = []
    used = [False] * len(nums)

    def backtrack(current: List[int]) -> None:
        if len(current) == len(nums):
            result.append(list(current))
            return
        for i, num in enumerate(nums):
            if not used[i]:
                used[i] = True
                current.append(num)
                backtrack(current)
                current.pop()
                used[i] = False

    backtrack([])
    return result
